subtasks.title: Backend: Express server bootstrap + health endpoint
subtasks.description: Create src/server.js with Express app, CORS config (allow frontend origin), JSON body parser, /api/health endpoint, and listen on PORT env var (default 3001). Add 'dev' and 'start' scripts to package.json.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/health returns { status: 'ok' } with 200
CORS allows requests from http://localhost:3000
Server listens on PORT env var, defaults to 3001
npm run dev starts the server with --watch flag (Node 18+ native)

subtasks.title: Backend: PDF upload endpoint with text extraction
subtasks.description: Create POST /api/upload using multer (memory storage, single file, 20MB limit). Accept multipart/form-data with field 'file'. Extract text via pdf-parse. Store extracted text in an in-memory Map keyed by a generated sessionId (uuid or nanoid-style). Return sessionId + metadata (filename, page count, character count). Reject non-PDF files.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/upload accepts multipart/form-data with 'file' field
Rejects files that are not application/pdf with 400 status
Rejects files over 20MB with 413 status
Extracts text using pdf-parse and stores in memory
Returns JSON: { sessionId, filename, pageCount, charCount }
sessionId is a unique string per upload

subtasks.title: Backend: Chat endpoint with Groq streaming (SSE)
subtasks.description: Create POST /api/chat that accepts { sessionId, message, history }. Look up extracted PDF text by sessionId. Build messages array: system prompt with PDF content, then conversation history, then user message. Call Groq API (llama-3.3-70b-versatile) with stream: true. Pipe response as SSE (text/event-stream). Each SSE event sends a JSON chunk with { content, done }. On completion, send final event with done: true.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/chat accepts JSON { sessionId, message, history }
Returns 404 if sessionId not found in memory
Returns 400 if message is empty
Sets Content-Type: text/event-stream
Streams Groq response token-by-token as SSE data events
Each SSE data line is valid JSON: { content: string, done: boolean }
Final event has done: true
System prompt includes the full extracted PDF text
Conversation history is passed to maintain context within session

subtasks.title: Backend: Error handling middleware + Groq API key validation
subtasks.description: Add global error handler middleware. On startup, check GROQ_API_KEY env var exists — log warning if missing. Handle Groq API errors (rate limit, invalid key, context too long) with appropriate HTTP status codes and user-friendly messages.
acceptance_criteria: Server logs warning on startup if GROQ_API_KEY is not set
Groq rate limit errors return 429 with retry message
Groq auth errors return 500 with 'API key invalid' message
Groq context length errors return 413 with 'PDF too large' message
Unhandled errors return 500 with generic message, no stack leak

subtasks.title: Frontend: Upload page with drag-and-drop
subtasks.description: Build the upload page at src/app/page.tsx. Full-viewport centered layout. Drop zone component with drag-and-drop support + click-to-browse fallback. Accept only .pdf files. Show file name after selection. Upload button that POSTs to backend /api/upload. Show loading spinner during upload. On success, redirect to /chat?sessionId=xxx. On error, show inline error message.
acceptance_criteria: Drop zone accepts drag-and-drop of PDF files
Click on drop zone opens file picker filtered to .pdf
Shows selected file name before upload
Upload button sends POST multipart/form-data to /api/upload
Loading state shown during upload (button disabled + spinner)
On success, navigates to /chat?sessionId=<id>&filename=<name>
On error (non-PDF, too large, server error), shows inline error message
Responsive layout works on mobile and desktop

subtasks.title: Frontend: Chat interface with streaming display
subtasks.description: Create src/app/chat/page.tsx. Read sessionId from URL search params. Chat layout: header with document name + 'Upload new' button, scrollable message area, fixed input bar at bottom. Messages show user (right-aligned) and AI (left-aligned) bubbles. On send, POST to /api/chat and consume SSE stream. Append tokens to the current AI message in real-time. Auto-scroll to bottom on new messages. Disable input while AI is responding.
acceptance_criteria: Reads sessionId from URL query params
Redirects to / if sessionId is missing
Header shows document filename and 'Upload new' link back to /
User messages appear right-aligned
AI messages appear left-aligned with different background
Sends POST to /api/chat with sessionId, message, and history
Consumes SSE stream and renders tokens incrementally
Auto-scrolls to latest message during streaming
Input is disabled while AI is streaming a response
Empty messages cannot be sent
Conversation history is maintained in component state

subtasks.title: Frontend: SSE client utility
subtasks.description: Create a reusable fetchSSE utility (src/lib/sse.ts) that POSTs JSON to an endpoint, reads the response as a ReadableStream, parses SSE 'data:' lines, and calls an onChunk callback with each parsed JSON object. Handle connection errors and abort support via AbortController.
acceptance_criteria: Sends POST with JSON body and reads streaming response
Parses SSE data: lines into JSON objects
Calls onChunk callback for each parsed chunk
Calls onDone callback when stream ends or done: true received
Calls onError callback on network or parse errors
Supports AbortController for cancellation

subtasks.title: Frontend: Empty state + welcome message in chat
subtasks.description: When chat loads with a valid sessionId but no messages yet, show an empty state: document icon, document name, and 3 suggested starter questions (e.g., 'Summarize this document', 'What are the main topics?', 'What are the key conclusions?'). Clicking a suggestion fills the input and sends it.
acceptance_criteria: Empty state shows when no messages exist yet
Displays document filename
Shows at least 3 clickable starter question chips
Clicking a chip sends that question as the first message
Empty state disappears after first message is sent

subtasks.title: Docker: Dockerfiles + docker-compose.yml
subtasks.description: Create backend/Dockerfile (Node 18 Alpine, copy package*.json, npm ci, copy src, expose 3001, CMD node src/server.js). Create frontend/Dockerfile (Node 18 Alpine, multi-stage: install deps, build Next.js, run standalone). Create docker-compose.yml at project root: backend service (port 3001, env GROQ_API_KEY from .env), frontend service (port 3000, depends_on backend, env NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL). Add .env.example with GROQ_API_KEY placeholder.
acceptance_criteria: docker compose up builds and starts both services
Backend accessible at localhost:3001
Frontend accessible at localhost:3000
Frontend can reach backend API
GROQ_API_KEY injected from .env file
.env.example exists with placeholder values

subtasks.title: Integration: End-to-end flow test (manual smoke test plan)
subtasks.description: Document the manual smoke test checklist: upload a PDF, verify redirect to chat, send a question, verify streaming response, send follow-up question to test conversation history, try uploading non-PDF (expect error), try uploading oversized file (expect error).
acceptance_criteria: Smoke test checklist covers happy path (upload → chat → stream)
Smoke test checklist covers error paths (wrong file type, too large)
Smoke test checklist covers conversation continuity (follow-up questions)

endpoints.description: Health check for readiness probes and frontend connectivity test
Upload a PDF file and extract its text content
Send a message about the uploaded document and receive a streaming AI response

errors.body: { "error": "Session not found. Please upload a PDF first." }
{ "error": "Rate limited. Please wait a moment and try again." }

parallel_group_1.description: Backend foundation + Frontend pages can be built in parallel

parallel_group_2.description: Docker + integration after both sides are done

assumptions: Groq free tier rate limits (30 req/min, 6000 tokens/min) are acceptable for single-user usage
PDF text extraction via pdf-parse is sufficient — no OCR needed for scanned PDFs
128k context window of llama-3.3-70b-versatile can handle most PDFs (roughly up to ~300 pages of text)
In-memory storage is acceptable — sessions are lost on server restart, which is fine for MVP
No authentication needed — single user, local use
Frontend sends conversation history in each request rather than backend storing it (stateless chat endpoint, simpler backend)
SSE is preferred over WebSocket for streaming — simpler, one-directional, sufficient for this use case
Node 18 Alpine is the Docker base image — matches Node.js native --watch support
Next.js standalone output mode is used for the frontend Docker image to minimize size
